results.editorial:
Pusat Warga Mas Penyayang — operating under the name Kuan Ai Pusat Warga Mas Penyayang — is an elderly care service located at Jalan 3/1 in Taman Serdang Jaya, Seri Kembangan, within the southern Selangor corridor near the Serdang–Kajang axis. The facility holds 40 beds according to directory records, making it a mid-sized community-oriented home in a township that sits equidistant from Serdang Hospital and the Seri Kembangan commercial belt. The name Kuan Ai carries a Hokkien charitable resonance, suggesting a community welfare background rather than a commercial chain.

Publicly available information about this facility is sparse. It is listed as an elderly care service in business directories, with a recorded operating schedule of Monday to Friday, 8 am to 5 pm. That weekday-only schedule is worth noting: it may indicate a day-care-primary model, a skeleton live-in arrangement, or simply that administrative and visiting hours are restricted to weekdays. The Google Maps rating sits at 3.6 from eight reviews — a modest score that warrants investigation. No website beyond a Facebook page linked to the Kuan Ai name is publicly maintained, which limits independent verification of care scope.

Families considering this centre should call directly to clarify whether it offers 24-hour residential nursing care or primarily day-care placement, and whether live-in staff are present overnight. Given the 40-bed capacity and community-welfare framing, pricing may be more accessible than commercial chains — but ask for written confirmation. The 3.6 rating on a small sample is not alarming but does suggest requesting references from current residents' families. Proximity to Serdang Hospital (a government teaching hospital) is a practical plus for medical emergencies.

facts.care_types: Elderly care service (day care or residential unclear)
facts.red_flags: Weekday-only listed hours suggest day-care model; 3.6 Google rating from small sample; very limited public information

results.editorial:
Love and Care Village is a nursing home located in Taman Ampang Utama, a residential estate in Ampang on the Selangor–KL border. The area sits in a well-connected part of greater Ampang, close to Jalan Ampang and within reach of Ampang Puteri Specialist Hospital and Hospital Ampang. The facility is listed on Google Maps as a nursing home with a 5-star rating from seven reviews — a perfect score that, on a small sample, reflects early positive feedback but should be read cautiously rather than as a definitive quality signal.

Beyond the Maps listing, Love and Care Village has no publicly accessible website or Facebook page that surfaced in research. No capacity figures, care programme details, pricing, or staffing information have been independently published. The name suggests a residential, village-style model oriented toward warm community living rather than a purely clinical nursing approach, though this cannot be confirmed from available sources. Contact is through a mobile number (+60 12-353 9035), which is typical of smaller, owner-operated homes.

Families should call to determine whether the facility caters to ambulant elderly, nursing-dependent residents, or both; what medical cover (if any) is on site or on-call; and whether a JKM licence is held. The Ampang Utama location, while residential and relatively quiet, may also be of interest to families based in Ampang, Wangsa Maju, or Keramat who want proximity to KL without the congestion of the city centre. Given the thin public footprint, an in-person visit before committing is strongly advisable.

facts.red_flags: No website or social media presence found; 5-star rating from only 7 reviews — small sample; all contact via mobile only

results.editorial:
Pusat Jagaan Nurul Jannah is a private elderly care centre established in 2018 and based at Jalan AU 5C/12 in Ampang Jaya, Selangor, on the KL border near Keramat and Wangsa Maju. It was founded with an explicitly Muslim-women-centred mission — the centre's stated tagline translates as "to care for, treat, educate, and show love to Muslim elderly women." This makes it a specialist placement for Malay-Muslim families seeking a faith-aligned residential setting for female relatives. The centre operates 24 hours, seven days a week, and is classified in directories as a home health care service.

The facility offers both monthly and daily placement, giving families flexibility for respite or long-term arrangements. Its website and Google Sites presence indicate that services include light medical treatment for unwell residents, religious classes for interested residents, and comprehensive emotional support. The dual contact model — separate numbers for male (En. Iqbal) and female (Pn. Yus) staff liaisons — reflects cultural sensitivity in communications. The Google Maps rating is 4.7 from seven reviews, suggesting consistent positive feedback from families who have used the service. Specific bed count, room types, and pricing are not publicly stated.

Key questions to raise on enquiry include current capacity and whether there is a waitlist, the level of nursing care available for residents with mobility limitations or medical devices, and how acute medical events are handled overnight. Given the female-only and Muslim-focused character, families should also confirm whether male caregivers are present and under what circumstances. The Jalan AU 5C/12 address in Ampang Jaya places it reasonably close to Hospital Ampang for emergencies.

facts.care_types: Home health care / residential elderly care (women only)
facts.red_flags: Female-only facility — confirm care scope for medically dependent residents; pricing not disclosed publicly

results.editorial:
Summer Breeze Cottage Caring Home occupies a renovated double-storey property at 11 Jalan 5/39 in Section 5, Bukit Gasing, Petaling Jaya — a leafy, low-density residential pocket on the edge of the Bukit Gasing forest reserve. The home is built on a 12,000 sq ft plot, giving it unusually generous outdoor space for a PJ nursing home, with a sizable backyard, an activity and dining patio, and two large indoor dining areas. The website positions it as upscale assisted living for mobile and semi-mobile seniors, with a Google Maps rating of 4.4 from seven reviews. Listed capacity is 25 beds.

The facility is staffed 24 hours with trained in-house caregivers and offers a programme that the website describes as physically and mentally stimulating. Listed services include physical therapy, assisted feeding, outdoor activities, and companionship programming. Short-term care and day-care arrangements are explicitly offered in addition to long-term residence, making it a reasonable respite option. Safety-equipped bathrooms with rails are noted, and both private and shared room configurations are available. Operating hours for visits are listed as 9:30 am to 7:00 pm daily, with the centre stating it is wheelchair accessible.

Pricing is not publicly disclosed, and there is no JKM licence number visible in available sources — families should ask directly about the licence status and request it in writing. With only 25 beds and a boutique positioning, availability may be tight; call ahead and book a tour early. The Bukit Gasing location is quiet but not immediately adjacent to a major hospital — the nearest specialists are in Damansara or PJ Seksyen 16, so families should factor travel time for medical appointments into their assessment.

facts.care_types: Assisted living, day care, short-term care
facts.red_flags: No licence number publicly visible; pricing undisclosed; small 25-bed boutique may have waitlist

results.editorial:
PJ Care Centre's Seksyen 14 branch is a deliberately low-density nursing home at 20 Jalan 14/30, in the mature Seksyen 14 neighbourhood of Petaling Jaya. The centre caps its occupancy at 13 residents across a 4,000-plus sq ft property — a choice that prioritises privacy and one-to-one attention over throughput. Room configurations range from 3-bed sharing to single en-suite options, giving families a meaningful range of privacy levels within a single facility. The Google Maps listing carries a 4.4 rating from seven reviews. PJ Care Centre also operates a second branch, meaning it has institutional continuity beyond a single-owner setup.

Care at Seksyen 14 includes 24-hour monitoring by qualified caregivers covering bathing, grooming, feeding, and mobility assistance. Daily home-cooked meals run from breakfast through dessert with fresh fruit, and dietary consultation is available. Medication management is handled by what the facility describes as a licensed geriatric pharmacist — an unusual and noteworthy capability for a home of this size. The outdoor garden produces organic fruit and vegetables and doubles as an activity space, with residents participating in group exercise, dancing, and mahjong. On-site resident nurses are mentioned, though exact nurse-to-resident ratios are not published.

With a 13-person cap, prospective families should expect a waitlist and should enquire early. Pricing is not advertised; given the upscale positioning and low density, fees are likely above mid-market. Clarify whether in-house nursing extends to residents requiring IV medication, wound care, or nasogastric tube management, as the published scope focuses more on quality-of-life programming than clinical complexity. The Seksyen 14 central location places it within a few minutes of Pantai Hospital Kuala Lumpur and PJ city amenities.

facts.red_flags: Max 13 residents — likely waitlist; pricing not disclosed; clinical scope for high-dependency residents unconfirmed

results.editorial:
Cherish Retirement and Nursing Home is located in PJS 3, the Jalan Klang Lama-adjacent stretch of Petaling Jaya that falls under the broader Taman Desasiswa corridor. The facility has a Google Maps rating of 4.3 from seven reviews and a listed capacity of 10 beds, indicating a very small, intimate home rather than a conventional multi-ward nursing facility. The phone number 03-7773 2333 is active in directory listings, and a Facebook page exists for the facility. The small bed count and PJS 3 address suggest a converted residential property operating as a boutique retirement home.

Publicly available information about Cherish Retirement's care scope, staffing, and programmes is limited. No dedicated website was found beyond the Facebook presence, and care-type details are not confirmed in independent listings. For a 10-bed facility in a residential neighbourhood, the model is likely one of personalised attention with a small caregiver team; what clinical capabilities exist on-site — particularly for nursing-dependent or post-hospitalisation residents — is unknown from public sources. The 4.3 Google rating is positive on this small sample but not based on a high review volume.

Families exploring Cherish should visit in person and ask directly: whether a JKM licence is held and current; what the nurse or caregiver-to-resident ratio is; how medical emergencies are managed; and whether there are visiting hours or an open-door policy. The PJS 3 location is practical for families in Petaling Jaya, Bangsar, and Old Klang Road, with several private hospitals within a 10–15 minute drive. Given the 10-bed size, availability is likely limited and advance enquiry is essential.

facts.red_flags: Only 10 beds — very limited capacity; no website found; care scope and licence status unverified

results.editorial:
Noble Care Malaysia Sdn Bhd operates a nursing home branch at No. 24-1, Subang Business Centre, Jalan USJ 9/5T in USJ 9, Subang Jaya — one of several branches the Noble Care chain runs across Klang Valley and beyond. Being part of a managed chain rather than a single-owner home means the USJ 9 branch operates under standardised protocols, a corporate governance structure, and multi-location operational experience. The facility has a Google Maps rating of 3.9 from seven reviews. Contact is via mobile (+60 16-278 6993), and the corporate website at mynoblecare.com carries detailed information about the group's services.

The Noble Care group website lists an extensive clinical capability set that applies across its branches, including dementia and Alzheimer's care, palliative care, respite care, stroke and cancer management, post-operative care, and specialist nursing procedures such as tracheostomy care, Foley catheter management, nasogastric tube changes, colostomy bag changing, and bedsore dressing. Physiotherapy is also listed. This positions Noble Care USJ 9 at the higher-dependency end of the nursing home spectrum — more clinical than most boutique homes in the area. Home nursing is additionally available for families not yet ready for residential placement.

The 3.9 Google rating is the group's USJ 9 score specifically and sits marginally below the group's other branches, worth bearing in mind. Bed count and pricing for the USJ 9 branch are not published; given the business-centre address (a shophouse unit rather than a landed property), the physical environment will differ from campus-style competitors. Families should ask about capacity, room configurations, the specific clinical staff on duty at USJ 9 versus centralised services, and whether the branch holds its own JKM licence or operates under a group licence.

facts.care_types: Nursing home, dementia care, palliative care, respite care, post-operative care, physiotherapy, home nursing
facts.red_flags: 3.9 Google rating — below Noble Care's other branches; shophouse unit may limit space; group licence vs branch licence worth clarifying

results.editorial:
Sree Sai Elder Home Care Mental Health and Old Age Residential Home is located in Kuala Ampang, the older residential pocket of Ampang on the Selangor–KL border. The full name explicitly references mental health care alongside general elderly residential care, which is unusual and significant: most Malaysian nursing homes either omit mental health entirely or offer only basic dementia support. This naming suggests the facility may cater to elderly residents with psychiatric conditions, behavioural challenges, or advanced dementia, though the exact clinical scope requires direct confirmation. The facility carries a 5-star Google Maps rating from six reviews.

A website at sreesaioldagehome.com was identified in search results but was not accessible for full review at the time of research. Published web references describe services including professional elder care, nursing support, physiotherapy, post-surgical care, and assistance with daily routines. The Indian-origin name Sree Sai — referencing the devotional figure Sai Baba — suggests a likely Hindu or South Indian community connection, which may be relevant for Tamil-speaking families or those seeking a culturally familiar environment. Contact is through a mobile number (+60 12-297 0597).

Families should ask directly about the mental health care component: what conditions the facility accepts (schizophrenia, bipolar disorder, advanced dementia, behavioural BPSD), whether a psychiatrist or psychologist is involved in resident care, and what the staffing ratio is for behavioural support. Bed count, pricing, and JKM licence details are not publicly available and must be confirmed by telephone. The Kuala Ampang location is close to Hospital Ampang and within reasonable distance of the mental health facilities at Hospital Bahagia Ulu Kinta's outreach network.

facts.care_types: Old age residential home, mental health elderly care
facts.red_flags: Mental health scope not independently verified; website inaccessible during research; 5-star rating from only 6 reviews — small sample

results.editorial:
Oasis Nursing Home's Setia Alam branch is located at 66 Jalan Setia Impian U13/8B in the Setia Alam township, Shah Alam — a double-storey corner house within one of Selangor's best-planned residential developments. The facility is one of three Oasis branches operating across the greater Klang Valley, alongside locations in Shah Alam U12 and Klang, giving the group operational depth that single-location homes lack. The lead physician across the Oasis network is Dr. Wendy Oon, and the Setia Alam site shares the same medical and nursing leadership as the group's other branches. A 5-star Google Maps rating from six reviews reflects early strong feedback.

The Oasis group website lists a clear clinical remit across its branches: long-term care, short-term and post-hospitalisation care, stroke rehabilitation, palliative care, and dementia care. Named staff include a physiotherapist (Nur Ain) and nursing leads (Shaziq as Head Nurse, Siti Ramlah as Assistant Head Nurse), indicating a structured clinical hierarchy rather than an informally run home. The 24-hour care model with physician oversight makes this positioning closer to a nursing facility than an assisted-living centre. Phone contact is via mobile (+60 12-270 3166) and email at oasisnursingcare@gmail.com.

Bed count and pricing for the Setia Alam branch specifically are not published. As a corner double-storey house, physical capacity will be modest — families should call ahead to check availability. The Setia Alam township location is an advantage for residents from Shah Alam, Klang, or Subang who want a new, well-serviced suburb setting, but the nearest major hospital (Hospital Shah Alam) is approximately 10–15 minutes by car. Confirm whether the named physician conducts regular site visits at the Setia Alam branch specifically, and ask for the JKM registration number.

facts.care_types: Nursing home, post-hospitalisation care, stroke rehab, palliative care, dementia care
facts.red_flags: Capacity not stated — house-format likely small; confirm physician visit frequency at Setia Alam branch specifically

results.editorial:
My Peaceful Home Care Centre is a Subang Jaya-based nursing home established in 2010, with a second location opened in early 2022. The main facility sits at 14 Jalan SS 19/3 in SS 19, a mature residential pocket of Subang Jaya within a 15-minute drive of three major private hospitals — Sunway Medical Centre, Subang Jaya Medical Centre, and KPJ Selangor Specialist Hospital. The 7,200 sq ft compound provides meaningful space by SS 19 terrace-house standards, with a large backyard and accessibility features including handrails and powered wheelchair provision. The Google Maps rating is 4.8 from six reviews.

Care at My Peaceful Home is described as 24-hour, with multiple caregivers on-site and a weekly in-house physician examination for residents. The facility explicitly accepts residents with dementia and Alzheimer's disease and other degenerative conditions. Room configurations include single, twin-sharing, and multi-sharing options, each with personal call-bell systems. Activity programming covers physical exercise regimens designed to address muscle atrophy and coordination decline, as well as cognitive stimulation through puzzles and themed festive events. The centre has been operating for over 15 years, giving it one of the longer track records among smaller Subang Jaya homes.

Pricing is not published and must be obtained by enquiry. Families should ask about nurse versus caregiver staffing ratios specifically — the published description emphasises caregivers and a visiting physician, so it is worth confirming whether a resident nurse is on duty overnight and what clinical procedures (e.g. wound care, catheter management, PEG feeding) can be handled in-house. The SS 19 address is convenient for families in Subang Jaya, USJ, and Petaling Jaya, and the proximity to multiple specialists is a practical safety net for medically complex residents.

facts.care_types: Nursing home, dementia care, assisted living
facts.red_flags: Overnight nursing cover not explicitly confirmed; pricing undisclosed; clinical procedure scope (wound care, PEG) not stated
